annotations.label: Topological group

anchor.snippet: A topological group , G , is a topological space that is also a group such that the group operation

annotations.note: Mathlib's `IsTopologicalGroup` is the class for a group with continuous multiplication and inversion.

annotations.label: Group topology

anchor.snippet: Such a topology is said to be compatible with the group operations and is called a group topology

annotations.note: `GroupTopology α` is the bundled type of topologies on `α` making it a topological group.

annotations.label: Continuity of product map

anchor.snippet: The product map is continuous if and only if for any

annotations.note: Continuity of multiplication is the typeclass `ContinuousMul`, but the explicit pointwise reformulation in nhds is not stated as an iff.

annotations.label: Continuity of inversion map

anchor.snippet: The inversion map is continuous if and only if for any

annotations.note: Continuity of inversion is the typeclass `ContinuousInv`, but the nhds reformulation is not stated as an iff.

annotations.label: Sufficient condition for compatibility

anchor.snippet: To show that a topology is compatible with the group operations, it suffices to check that the map

annotations.note: Mathlib provides constructors like `IsTopologicalGroup.of_nhds_one` rather than the specific `(x,y) ↦ x*y⁻¹` continuity criterion.

annotations.label: Homomorphism of topological groups

anchor.snippet: A homomorphism of topological groups is defined to be a continuous group homomorphism

annotations.note: `ContinuousMonoidHom A B` is the bundled type of continuous monoid homomorphisms.

annotations.label: Continuity from continuity at a point

anchor.snippet: A group homomorphism between topological groups is continuous if and only if it is continuous at some point

annotations.note: A monoid hom between topological groups is continuous iff it is continuous at the identity.

annotations.label: Isomorphism of topological groups

anchor.snippet: An isomorphism of topological groups is a group isomorphism that is also a homeomorphism of the underlying topological spaces

annotations.note: `ContinuousMulEquiv` extends `MulEquiv` and `Homeomorph`, exactly Wikipedia's definition.

annotations.label: Discrete vs indiscrete topology on a group

anchor.snippet: For example, let G be any group. We can put at least two topologies on it

annotations.note: An informal example rather than a Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Discrete groups

anchor.snippet: Every group can be trivially made into a topological group by considering it with the discrete topology

annotations.note: Mathlib derives `IsTopologicalGroup` from discrete topology via the general `ContinuousMul`/`ContinuousInv` instances but no single decl named for the example.

annotations.label: Real numbers and Euclidean space as topological groups

anchor.snippet: The group of real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has instances making `ℝ` and `ℝⁿ` topological additive groups.

annotations.label: General linear group

anchor.snippet: The classical groups are important examples of non-abelian topological groups

annotations.note: `GL n R` is defined and equipped with topology in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal group

anchor.snippet: Another classical group is the orthogonal group

annotations.note: `Matrix.orthogonalGroup n R` is defined as an abbreviation for `unitaryGroup`.

annotations.label: Lie groups

anchor.snippet: The groups mentioned so far are all Lie groups , meaning that they are smooth manifolds

annotations.note: `LieGroup I n G` is the class of Lie groups in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rationals and p-adic integers

anchor.snippet: An example of a topological group that is not a Lie group is the additive group

annotations.note: `PadicInt` is defined as the ring of p-adic integers carrying its valuation topology in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Profinite groups

anchor.snippet: is a profinite group ; it is isomorphic to a subgroup of the product

annotations.note: `ProfiniteGrp` is the category of profinite groups in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Infinite dimensional Lie groups

anchor.snippet: Some topological groups can be viewed as infinite dimensional Lie groups

annotations.note: Infinite-dimensional Lie groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invertible elements of Banach algebra

anchor.snippet: In every Banach algebra with multiplicative identity, the set of invertible elements forms a topological group under multiplication

annotations.note: Mathlib gives topological group structure on `Mˣ` for topological monoids satisfying continuity hypotheses; the Banach-algebra case is an instance of this.

annotations.label: Translation invariance of topology

anchor.snippet: Every topological group's topology is translation invariant

annotations.note: Left and right translations are homeomorphisms via `Homeomorph.mulLeft` and `Homeomorph.mulRight`.

annotations.label: Inversion is a homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: The inversion operation

annotations.note: `Homeomorph.inv G` packages inversion as a self-homeomorphism of `G`.

annotations.label: Symmetric subset

anchor.snippet: A subset

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsSymmetricRel` for uniform-space entourages but no dedicated `Set.IsSymmetric` for group subsets.

annotations.label: Symmetric neighborhood basis

anchor.snippet: For any neighborhood N in a commutative topological group G of the identity element, there exists a symmetric neighborhood M of the identity element

annotations.note: No specific decl for existence of symmetric `M ⊆ N` with `MM ⊆ N` in a topological group was found.

annotations.label: Relatively compact symmetric neighborhood

anchor.snippet: If G is a locally compact commutative group, then for any neighborhood N in G of the identity element, there exists a symmetric relatively compact neighborhood

annotations.note: No direct statement of a symmetric relatively compact neighborhood basis of the identity was found.

annotations.label: Left and right uniformities

anchor.snippet: Every topological group can be viewed as a uniform space in two ways

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IsTopologicalGroup.rightUniformSpace` and `IsTopologicalGroup.leftUniformSpace`.

annotations.label: Compact set absorbed by neighborhood

anchor.snippet: If U is an open subset of a commutative topological group G and U contains a compact set K , then there exists a neighborhood N of the identity element

annotations.note: No direct lemma of the form `K ⊆ U open ⇒ ∃ N ∈ 𝓝 1, K·N ⊆ U` was found.

annotations.label: Equivalence of separation axioms for topological groups

anchor.snippet: As a uniform space, every commutative topological group is completely regular

annotations.note: Mathlib proves `RegularSpace` for any topological group but does not bundle the full T0 ⇔ T2 ⇔ T3 ⇔ T3½ equivalence.

annotations.label: Discrete subgroup iff isolated point

anchor.snippet: A subgroup of a commutative topological group is discrete if and only if it has an isolated point

annotations.note: No direct iff-with-isolated-point characterization of discreteness for subgroups was found.

annotations.label: Hausdorff quotient by closure of identity

anchor.snippet: If G is not Hausdorff, then one can obtain a Hausdorff group by passing to the quotient group

annotations.note: Mathlib's `SeparationQuotient` gives a T2 group quotient in the additive case; the multiplicative `G / closure {1}` version is not explicitly named.

annotations.label: Metrisable topological group

anchor.snippet: is metrisable if and only if there exists a metric

annotations.note: General metrisability is in Mathlib, but not a dedicated decl for group metrisability.

annotations.label: Left/right-invariant metric

anchor.snippet: left-invariant (resp. right-invariant ) if and only if

annotations.note: No `LeftInvariantMetric` typeclass was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Proper metric

anchor.snippet: proper if and only if all open balls,

annotations.note: Mathlib's `ProperSpace` captures the same condition (closed balls compact) at the level of metric spaces.

annotations.label: Birkhoff–Kakutani theorem

anchor.snippet: The Birkhoff–Kakutani theorem (named after mathematicians Garrett Birkhoff and Shizuo Kakutani ) states that the following three conditions

annotations.note: The Birkhoff–Kakutani metrisation theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Proper metrisability equivalences

anchor.snippet: Furthermore, the following are equivalent for any topological group

annotations.note: The proper-metrisability equivalence (Struble's theorem) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Subgroup is a topological group

anchor.snippet: Every subgroup of a topological group is itself a topological group when given the subspace topology

annotations.note: Mathlib has `instance (S : Subgroup G) : IsTopologicalGroup S`.

annotations.label: Open subgroup is closed

anchor.snippet: Every open subgroup H is also closed in G

annotations.note: `OpenSubgroup.isClosed` states an open subgroup is automatically closed.

annotations.label: Closure of subgroup is subgroup

anchor.snippet: If H is a subgroup of G , then the closure of H is also a subgroup

annotations.note: `Subgroup.topologicalClosure` packages the topological closure of a subgroup as a subgroup.

annotations.label: Closure of normal subgroup is normal

anchor.snippet: if H is a normal subgroup of G , the closure of H is normal in G

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the topological closure of a normal subgroup is normal.

annotations.label: Homogeneous space G/H

anchor.snippet: If H is a subgroup of G , the set of left cosets

annotations.note: `G ⧸ H` is given the quotient topology in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quotient map is open

anchor.snippet: The quotient map

annotations.note: `QuotientGroup.isOpenQuotientMap_mk` shows the canonical map is an open quotient map.

annotations.label: Sphere as homogeneous space

anchor.snippet: for a positive integer n , the sphere S n is a homogeneous space for the rotation group

annotations.note: The identification `S^n ≃ SO(n+1)/SO(n)` is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: G/H Hausdorff iff H closed

anchor.snippet: A homogeneous space G / H is Hausdorff if and only if H is closed in G

annotations.note: Mathlib proves T2 when H is closed; the converse direction is implicit via `t1Space_iff`.

annotations.label: Quotient group is topological group

anchor.snippet: If H is a normal subgroup of G , then the quotient group G / H becomes a topological group when given the quotient topology

annotations.note: Mathlib gives `G ⧸ N` an `IsTopologicalGroup` instance when `N.Normal`.

annotations.label: Identity component is closed normal subgroup

anchor.snippet: In any topological group, the identity component

annotations.note: The identity component is defined as a subgroup; closedness follows from `isClosed_connectedComponent` but the full closed+normal bundling is not packaged as one decl.

annotations.label: Product of compact and closed sets is closed

anchor.snippet: In any commutative topological group, the product (assuming the group is multiplicative) KC of a compact set K and a closed set C is a closed set

annotations.note: `IsClosed.mul_left_of_isCompact` (and `mul_right_of_isCompact`) give this for topological groups.

annotations.label: Closure product inclusion

anchor.snippet: for any subsets R and S of G

annotations.note: The closure-of-product inclusion is covered via `smul_set_closure_subset` and related pointwise closure lemmas in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closedness criterion for subgroup

anchor.snippet: If H is a subgroup of a commutative topological group G and if N is a neighborhood in G of the identity element

annotations.note: No direct decl matching this neighborhood-based closedness criterion for subgroups was found.

annotations.label: Discrete subgroup is closed

anchor.snippet: Every discrete subgroup of a Hausdorff commutative topological group is closed

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Subgroup.isClosed_of_discrete` for T2 topological groups.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem for topological groups

anchor.snippet: There is a version of the first isomorphism theorem for topological groups

annotations.note: Algebraic first isomorphism theorem exists; a topological-group version with a continuity/openness clause is not packaged as a single decl.

annotations.label: Continuous Lie group homomorphism is smooth

anchor.snippet: every continuous homomorphism of Lie groups

annotations.note: Automatic smoothness of continuous Lie group homomorphisms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cartan's theorem

anchor.snippet: Cartan's theorem says that every closed subgroup of a Lie group is a Lie subgroup

annotations.note: Cartan's closed subgroup theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hilbert's fifth problem (Gleason–Montgomery–Zippin)

anchor.snippet: Hilbert's fifth problem asked whether a topological group G that is a topological manifold must be a Lie group

annotations.note: The Gleason–Montgomery–Zippin solution to Hilbert's fifth problem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compact groups are inverse limits of compact Lie groups

anchor.snippet: every compact group (understood to be Hausdorff) is an inverse limit of compact Lie groups

annotations.note: This inverse-limit structure theorem for compact groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Connected locally compact groups are inverse limits of Lie groups

anchor.snippet: every connected locally compact group is an inverse limit of connected Lie groups

annotations.note: This Yamabe-type structure theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Totally disconnected locally compact group contains compact open subgroup

anchor.snippet: a totally disconnected locally compact group always contains a compact open subgroup

annotations.note: Related infrastructure on totally disconnected/nonarchimedean groups exists but van Dantzig's theorem is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Action of a topological group

anchor.section: Representations of compact or locally compact groups
anchor.snippet: An action of a topological group G on a topological space X is a group action of G on X such that the corresponding function

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `ContinuousSMul` for jointly continuous group actions.

annotations.label: Representation of a topological group

anchor.section: Representations of compact or locally compact groups
anchor.snippet: a representation of a topological group G on a real or complex topological vector space V is a continuous action of G on V

annotations.note: Continuous representations on topological vector spaces are expressed via `ContinuousSMul` plus module structure; no dedicated `Representation` typeclass for topological groups.

annotations.label: Finite-dim representation decomposes as direct sum

anchor.section: Representations of compact or locally compact groups
anchor.snippet: every finite-dimensional (real or complex) representation of a compact group is a direct sum of irreducible representations

annotations.note: Complete reducibility of finite-dimensional representations of compact groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Peter–Weyl theorem

anchor.section: Representations of compact or locally compact groups
anchor.snippet: An infinite-dimensional unitary representation of a compact group can be decomposed as a Hilbert-space direct sum of irreducible representations

annotations.note: The Peter–Weyl theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Irreducible representations of the circle group

anchor.section: Representations of compact or locally compact groups
anchor.snippet: the theory of Fourier series describes the decomposition of the unitary representation of the circle group

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes Fourier series on `AddCircle` but not the explicit interpretation as irreducible representations.

annotations.label: Weyl character formula

anchor.section: Representations of compact or locally compact groups
anchor.snippet: the character of each irreducible representation is given by the Weyl character formula

annotations.note: The Weyl character formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Direct integral decomposition for locally compact groups

anchor.section: Representations of compact or locally compact groups
anchor.snippet: Every unitary representation of a locally compact group can be described as a direct integral of irreducible unitary representations

annotations.note: Direct integral decomposition of unitary representations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fourier transform as direct integral

anchor.section: Representations of compact or locally compact groups
anchor.snippet: A basic example is the Fourier transform , which decomposes the action of the additive group

annotations.note: The Fourier transform viewed as a direct-integral decomposition of the regular representation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pontryagin duality

anchor.section: Representations of compact or locally compact groups
anchor.snippet: Pontryagin duality states that for a locally compact abelian group G , the dual of

annotations.note: `PontryaginDual` is defined and the duality is established for finite abelian groups, but full LCA Pontryagin duality is not.

annotations.label: Gelfand–Raikov theorem

anchor.section: Representations of compact or locally compact groups
anchor.snippet: Every locally compact group G has a good supply of irreducible unitary representations

annotations.note: The Gelfand–Raikov theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classifying space and loop space

anchor.snippet: a topological group G determines a path-connected topological space, the classifying space

annotations.note: Classifying spaces `BG` of topological groups are not formalized.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of topological group is abelian

anchor.snippet: the fundamental group of a topological group G is abelian

annotations.note: Commutativity of `π₁` of a topological group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cohomology ring is a Hopf algebra

anchor.snippet: for any field k , the cohomology ring

annotations.note: The Hopf algebra structure on `H*(G; k)` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Structure of rational cohomology ring

anchor.snippet: if G is a path-connected topological group whose rational cohomology ring

annotations.note: Hopf's theorem on the rational cohomology ring of a Lie group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Maximal compact subgroup of connected Lie group

anchor.snippet: a connected Lie group G has a maximal compact subgroup K , which is unique up to conjugation

annotations.note: Existence and uniqueness of maximal compact subgroups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classification of compact connected Lie groups

anchor.snippet: compact connected Lie groups have been classified by Wilhelm Killing

annotations.note: The Killing–Cartan classification of compact connected Lie groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Diagonal and canonical entourage

anchor.section: Canonical uniformity on a commutative topological group
anchor.snippet: The diagonal of

annotations.note: Diagonal and entourages are defined generally; the group-specific canonical entourages from `N ∋ 1` are folded into `IsTopologicalGroup.rightUniformSpace`.

annotations.label: Canonical uniformity

anchor.section: Canonical uniformity on a commutative topological group
anchor.snippet: the canonical uniformity

annotations.note: Mathlib's `IsTopologicalGroup.rightUniformSpace` (and `leftUniformSpace`) realizes the canonical uniformity.

annotations.label: Translation-invariant uniformity

anchor.section: Canonical uniformity on a commutative topological group
anchor.snippet: a fundamental system of entourages

annotations.note: No explicit `TranslationInvariantUniformity` typeclass was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Canonical uniformity is translation-invariant

anchor.section: Canonical uniformity on a commutative topological group
anchor.snippet: The canonical uniformity on any commutative topological group is translation-invariant

annotations.note: Translation-invariance of the canonical uniformity is not stated as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Product, sum, and difference of nets

anchor.snippet: Make

annotations.note: Mathlib works with filters rather than directly defining pointwise products of nets in topological groups.

annotations.label: Cauchy net

anchor.snippet: A net

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Cauchy` is defined for filters; net Cauchy-ness is captured via the associated filter.

annotations.label: Cauchy sequence

anchor.snippet: A Cauchy sequence is a Cauchy net that is a sequence

annotations.note: `CauchySeq` is Mathlib's definition of a Cauchy sequence in a uniform space.

annotations.label: N-small set

anchor.snippet: is said to be an

annotations.note: No group-specific `N-small` set definition was found; Mathlib instead uses entourage-small via `Set.Pairwise` and uniformity.

annotations.label: Cauchy prefilter

anchor.snippet: A prefilter

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Cauchy` on filters subsumes the prefilter notion.

annotations.label: Convergence iff adherent and Cauchy

anchor.snippet: Suppose

annotations.note: Standard uniform-space lemmas state that a Cauchy filter converges iff it has a cluster point.

annotations.label: Complete subset

anchor.snippet: of a topological group

annotations.note: `IsComplete s` defines completeness of a subset in a uniform space.

annotations.label: Sequentially complete subset

anchor.snippet: is called a sequentially complete subset if every Cauchy sequence

annotations.note: Mathlib speaks of `SequentiallyComplete` for spaces but not as a subset-relative predicate for groups.

annotations.label: Complete group

anchor.snippet: A commutative topological group

annotations.note: Completeness of a topological group uses the general `CompleteSpace` typeclass on the associated uniform space.

annotations.label: Sequentially complete topological group

anchor.snippet: A topological group is called sequentially complete if it is a sequentially complete subset of itself

annotations.note: `SequentiallyComplete` exists as a general uniform-space notion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Neighborhood basis of a completion

anchor.snippet: Neighborhood basis

annotations.note: An explicit neighborhood basis description of the group completion is not given as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Uniform continuity between topological groups

anchor.snippet: is uniformly continuous if for every neighborhood

annotations.note: `UniformContinuous` is Mathlib's general definition specialized to the group uniformity.

annotations.label: Semitopological group

anchor.snippet: A semitopological group is a group G with a topology such that for each

annotations.note: Separate continuity of left/right translation is captured via `ContinuousConstSMul` but `SemitopologicalGroup` itself is not defined.

annotations.label: Quasitopological group

anchor.snippet: A quasitopological group is a semitopological group in which the function mapping elements to their inverses is also continuous

annotations.note: Quasitopological groups are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Paratopological group

anchor.snippet: A paratopological group is a group with a topology such that the group operation is continuous

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `Group` + `ContinuousMul` for this notion but has no dedicated `ParatopologicalGroup` name.
